metadata.sources: DASMA wind-load standards, IDA-C contractor surveys, manufacturer trade pricing (Clopay, Wayne Dalton, Amarr, CHI, Haas), RSMeans residential 2026, BBB garage door complaint patterns

doorTiers.label: Value steel, uninsulated
doorTiers.notes: Single-layer 25-gauge steel. Cheap, lightweight, dent-prone. Acceptable for detached garages.

doorTiers.label: Mid-range insulated steel
doorTiers.notes: Two-layer with polystyrene foam. Standard for attached garages. Choose insulated whenever the garage shares a wall with conditioned space.

doorTiers.label: Premium insulated steel
doorTiers.notes: Three-layer with polyurethane (better insulator than polystyrene). Quieter, stronger, less dent-prone.

doorTiers.label: Carriage house faux-wood
doorTiers.notes: Steel with composite overlays for wood look. The most popular premium upgrade in 2026 remodels. Adds significant curb appeal.

doorTiers.label: Real wood
doorTiers.notes: Custom-built. Hemlock, cedar, mahogany. Highest cost. Requires periodic refinishing.

doorTiers.label: Aluminum and glass (full view)
doorTiers.notes: Modern look. Common for garage-to-living conversions and showroom doors.

openerTiers.label: Chain drive (basic)
openerTiers.notes: Cheapest. Loudest. Acceptable for detached garages.

openerTiers.label: Wall-mount jackshaft
openerTiers.notes: Mounts on the side wall — frees up ceiling space. Best for high-ceiling or storage garages.

openerTiers.label: Smart connected (myQ, HomeLink)
openerTiers.notes: WiFi, smartphone control, package delivery integration. Standard on premium tier.

single_door_replacement_value.label: Single 9x7 value steel door + opener install

single_door_replacement_mid.label: Single 9x7 mid insulated steel door + opener install

double_door_replacement_mid.label: Double 16x7 mid insulated steel + opener install

double_door_replacement_premium.label: Double 16x7 premium insulated steel + smart belt drive

double_door_carriage_house.label: Double 16x7 carriage house style + smart opener

double_door_real_wood.label: Double 16x7 real wood custom + smart opener

supportingComponents.item: Smart opener add-on (if existing opener is dumb)

redFlagPatterns.explanation: Garage door springs store enormous tension. Improper handling kills people every year. Spring replacement is NEVER a DIY job. If a quote is encouraging you to DIY this, walk away.
Garage door quoted with no brand or model. Quality varies wildly — same dimensions can be $600 or $5,500 depending on tier. Always specify.
Opener with no brand or HP rating named.

Coastal/hurricane-zone install with no wind rating specified. Coastal counties require DASMA 110-150+ wind ratings (Miami-Dade approved or TDI approved in coastal TX). Insurance won't pay wind claims on a non-rated door.

redFlagPatterns.regex: \b(attached garage|garage with bedroom above|garage to house|conditioned garage|finished garage|living above)\b
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Attached garage door quote with no insulation specified. Uninsulated doors cause significant heat loss and freeze damage to garage plumbing. R-12+ is the right baseline for attached garages.
Opener install with no mention of safety sensors. Photoelectric safety sensors are required by federal law (UL 325) on all garage door openers since 1993. Confirm in writing.
Deposit above 50% on a garage door is unusual. Most reputable installers cap at 25-50% with the balance on completion.
No warranty stated. Reputable installers offer separate door, opener, and labor warranties. Doors typically 10-25 years (panel/finish), openers 1-15 years, labor 1-5 years.
Springs sold without cycle rating. Standard springs are 10,000 cycles (~7 years for typical use); high-cycle springs are 20,000-50,000 cycles. Worth the upgrade for 2-car families.

scopeCheckItems.label: Opener brand, type, and HP rating
Old door tear-off and disposal included

stateLicensing.notes: Garage door installers are typically licensed under the state general contractor or specialty installer license.
stateLicensing.ida_certified: Voluntary IDA International Door Association certification — strong quality signal.

questionsToAskContractor: What door brand and model are you quoting?
What's the insulation R-value? (Critical for attached garages.)
If coastal: what's the wind rating? (DASMA / Miami-Dade / TDI)
What opener brand, type (chain/belt/jackshaft), and horsepower?
Are photoelectric safety sensors per UL 325 included?
What spring cycle rating — standard 10K or high-cycle 20K+?
Is bottom seal and weatherstripping included?
What are the door, opener, and labor warranties separately?
Will you remove and dispose of the old door?
Is this a smart opener? (myQ, HomeLink, or compatible)
